evals.prompt: In this TypeScript package, find dead code, unused imports, and duplicate helpers. Remove only the safe items and verify after each deletion.
evals.expected_output: Selects a suitable analyzer or ripgrep fallback, classifies findings into SAFE/CAUTION/DANGER, deletes one item at a time, reruns verification after each deletion, and summarizes what was removed, skipped, and left uncertain.
assertions: Explains which analyzer or fallback search it would use for the repo.
Classifies candidates into SAFE, CAUTION, and DANGER before deleting anything.
Runs a baseline verification target before the first deletion.
Deletes one item at a time and reruns verification after each deletion.
Summarizes deleted, skipped, and remaining uncertain items.
expectations: Explains which analyzer or fallback search it would use for the repo.
Classifies candidates into SAFE, CAUTION, and DANGER before deleting anything.
Runs a baseline verification target before the first deletion.
Deletes one item at a time and reruns verification after each deletion.
Summarizes deleted, skipped, and remaining uncertain items.

evals.prompt: Clean up a published package with orphaned files and duplicate wrappers, but stop if anything could be part of the public API.
evals.expected_output: Checks for dynamic references and public exports before deleting risky items, removes duplicates only after the cleanup pass, and clearly explains what was left alone and why.
assertions: Searches for dynamic imports, string references, public exports, or generated entrypoints before deleting caution items.
Refuses to delete DANGER items without proof.
Cleans duplicates only after the dead-code pass.
Does not broaden the task into unrelated refactors.
Reports what was left alone and why.
expectations: Searches for dynamic imports, string references, public exports, or generated entrypoints before deleting caution items.
Refuses to delete DANGER items without proof.
Cleans duplicates only after the dead-code pass.
Does not broaden the task into unrelated refactors.
Reports what was left alone and why.

evals.prompt: Use whatever dead-code tool fits best, then clean the dead code in a Python-heavy repo and verify the change.
evals.expected_output: Chooses a stack-appropriate analyzer or ripgrep fallback, removes safe dead code incrementally, reruns verification after each deletion, and ends with a concise cleanup summary.
assertions: Chooses a stack-appropriate analyzer or ripgrep fallback.
Reruns the same test or build command after each deletion.
Does not refactor beyond the cleanup task.
Mentions the exact verification command(s) it ran.
Ends with a concise cleanup summary.

Does not refactor beyond the cleanup task.

Ends with a concise cleanup summary.
